claim: Socket Mode uses an outbound WebSocket so the app needs no public HTTP endpoint and is firewall-friendly, but it caps at 10 concurrent connections per app and Slack recommends HTTP for production and Marketplace apps.
quote: Socket Mode is a great option if you're building an on-premise integration, have no ability to receive external HTTP requests... Slack limits the number of concurrent WebSocket connections to 10 per app... we recommend using HTTP for production applications.

claim: Apps must respond to an Events API delivery with a 2xx within 3 seconds; Slack retries up to three times (immediately, +1 min, +5 min) with x-slack-retry-num, and >95% failures in 60 min temporarily disables the subscription.
quote: Your app should respond to the event request with an HTTP 2xx within three seconds... The first retry will be sent nearly immediately. The second retry will be attempted after 1 minute. The third and final retry will be sent after 5 minutes... more than 95% of delivery attempts within 60 minutes.

claim: Slack signs every HTTP request with HMAC-SHA256 over v0:timestamp:body keyed by the signing secret, delivered in X-Slack-Signature with X-Slack-Request-Timestamp, and receivers should reject timestamps older than 5 minutes to defeat replay attacks.
quote: Every Slack request includes X-Slack-Signature and X-Slack-Request-Timestamp headers... Reject requests older than 5 minutes to block replay attacks.

claim: Slash commands enforce the same 3-second budget as events: HTTP 200 within 3000 ms or Slack surfaces an operation_timeout error to the user.
quote: Commands must receive HTTP 200 responses within 3000 milliseconds of the original request, otherwise an operation_timeout error will be displayed to the user.

claim: The official Claude Code in Slack integration only triggers on @mentions in public or private channels (no DMs), gathers thread/channel context, runs the session on claude.ai/code, and offers Code-only vs Code+Chat routing modes with a Retry-as-Code escape hatch.
quote: Claude Code in Slack only works in channels (public or private). It does not work in direct messages (DMs)... a new Claude Code session is created on claude.ai/code... Code only — Claude routes all @mentions to Claude Code sessions... Code + Chat — Claude analyzes each message and intelligently routes.

claim: A common self-hosted pattern uses Slack Socket Mode with a connections:write app-level token so the bot needs no public ingress, running Claude Code locally against a configured working directory with per-thread working-directory state.
quote: Go to 'Basic Information' → 'App-Level Tokens' [and] Generate a token with connections:write scope... Thread-Specific Working Directories.

claim: Hardened bridges combine Socket Mode with an MCP stdio server spawned by Claude Code itself, providing per-thread session isolation, policy-gated tools, and a hash-chained audit journal across Bun/Node/Docker runtimes — all behind a firewall.
quote: WebSocket (Socket Mode — outbound only, no public URL)... server.ts (local MCP server, spawned by Claude Code)... per-thread session isolation.

claim: An alternative architecture puts a thin Slack handler (Cloudflare Workers + Hono) in front of GitHub — @mentions become GitHub issues, claude-code-action runs on the GitHub Actions runner, and a Cloudflare Queue polls issue/PR comments every 10 s for up to 30 minutes and mirrors them back to the Slack thread.
quote: Cloudflare Queues: Handles asynchronous progress monitoring, 'checking for GitHub updates every 10 seconds' and stopping 'after 30 minutes or when work is complete'.

claim: The canonical way to satisfy the 3-second ack while running long Claude Code work is to ack immediately and offload to a queue worker (SQS, Cloudflare Queue, Redis stream); processing inside the same HTTP handler is explicitly discouraged.
quote: Respond to events with an HTTP 200 OK as soon as you can. Avoid actually processing and reacting to events within the same process. Implement a queue to handle inbound events after they are received.

claim: For Events-API self-hosted bots without a static public IP, Cloudflare Tunnel has become the 2026 free default over ngrok because of stable hostnames that survive restarts and avoid Slack app reconfiguration.
quote: Custom persistent subdomains on the free tier allow you to no longer need to update your Stripe, GitHub, or Slack hook settings every time you restart your machine.
